AREA: Devon Cornwall and the Isles of Scilly
ImplementationScope: A scheme to improve long term confidence in Plymouth Bathing Waters (Plymouth Hoe East, and Plymouth Hoe West) achieving Sufficient. Forms the second part of a four phased approach to move towards 3 spills per bathing season aggregated performance atÿPlymouth Central STW CSO, Barbican Tanks CSO, and Cattedown Road SPS CSO/EO (Conoco Wharf).ÿ Phase 2 will deliver approximately 1/3rd of the performance improvement required at the Plymouth Central CSO.ÿ Using recent sewer network modelling, 1/3rd of the estimated design target will be achieved by delivery of ÿapproximately 20,000m3 additional storm storage at Plymouth Central STW, or improvements providing the performance equivalent, in accordance with the Plymouth long term strategy that was outlined in the report Plymouth Bathing Waters Stormwater Ultraviolet Disinfection Supporting Statement (dated November 2016). Specific measures to deliver the AMP7 outcome will be proposed by SWW during the planning phase for agreement. Agreement will rely on sewer modelling first predicting the improvement storage at Plymouth Central STW alone would provide and comparing that with the model predicted performance of the proposed combination of ÿmeasures. Delivery of the agreed specific measures will then be used as output measures for sign-off purposes. Recognising some measures may span AMPs and/or be varied, being reliant on third party cooperation, estimates of the beneficial performance delivered in each AMP may therefore need ÿremodelling to confirm sign-off. Ahead of PR24 EA and SWW will assess the benefit of the AMP7 improvements in line with the Plymouth long term strategy and the Storm Overflow Assessment Framework to agree what is needed for the next phase.
PropPermLimit: A scheme to achieve long term confidence around Plymouth Bathing Waters (Plymouth Hoe East, and Plymouth Hoe West) in achieving Excellent,  subject to willlingness to Pay. Forms part of a phased approach to deliver 3 spills solution by creating additional storage at Plymouth Central STW.
